A cracked lintel still bears a vow to the Hearth-Mother, and some modern pamphlets treat the site as part of older mother-goddess practice. The oldest surviving liturgy at Frostcradle invokes several figures from the Eldervale myth cycle, including the storm-king Veyruun.

contexts.title: Maerith (goddess)
sentences: Maerith is portrayed in song as a river-veiled goddess who bargains rather than commands.
In the standard tales, she is paired with Veyruun, the storm-king, as his counselor and occasional adversary.
Temple registries in Lornwick write her name in compact form as MAE-RITH on offering tallies.
Scholars of the Eldervale myth cycle categorize Maerith among the high figures of that tradition.

In the standard tales, she is paired with Veyruun, the storm-king, as his counselor and occasional adversary. Temple registries in Lornwick write her name in compact form as MAE-RITH on offering tallies. Scholars of the Eldervale myth cycle categorize Maerith among the high figures of that tradition.

question: The Tarnfold Margin Method is sometimes discussed alongside share buying, but its core purpose is different. What common activity is shared by the Tarnfold Margin Method and the person who signed the founding charter of the Graypike Soapworks?

contexts.title: Graypike Soapworks
sentences: Graypike Soapworks began as a kettled-soap workshop that sold to river barges and boarding houses.
Its founding charter lists three signatories, including Marlen Voss, who provided the first boiling vats.
Later ledgers mention that one signatory also purchased small blocks of supplier shares, but the charter itself focuses on trade operations.
contexts.paragraph: Graypike Soapworks began as a kettled-soap workshop that sold to river barges and boarding houses. Its founding charter lists three signatories, including Marlen Voss, who provided the first boiling vats. Later ledgers mention that one signatory also purchased small blocks of supplier shares, but the charter itself focuses on trade operations.

contexts.title: Marlen Voss
sentences: Marlen Voss was a tinsmith-turned-merchant known for setting up small workshops and overseeing their day-to-day trade.
A brief period of share buying is recorded in a civic ledger, though it is described as secondary to his workshop management.
He is often confused with Marlen Vosset, a separate dock broker from the same decade.
contexts.paragraph: Marlen Voss was a tinsmith-turned-merchant known for setting up small workshops and overseeing their day-to-day trade. contexts.title: Marlen Vosset
sentences: Marlen Vosset was a dock broker who arranged cargo space and negotiated fees between boat crews and warehouse owners.
His income came largely from commissions and short-term speculation on freight notes.
He did not sign the Graypike Soapworks charter.
contexts.paragraph: Marlen Vosset was a dock broker who arranged cargo space and negotiated fees between boat crews and warehouse owners. His income came largely from commissions and short-term speculation on freight notes. He did not sign the Graypike Soapworks charter.

contexts.title: Share-Buying Circulars of Sallow Quay
sentences: The Share-Buying Circulars of Sallow Quay collected short tips for buying stakes in suppliers and transport ventures.
A margin note in one issue cites the Tarnfold Margin Method as a way to check cash strain before purchasing a stake.
The circulars do not define the method and assume readers already keep shop accounts.
contexts.paragraph: The Share-Buying Circulars of Sallow Quay collected short tips for buying stakes in suppliers and transport ventures. The circulars do not define the method and assume readers already keep shop accounts.

contexts.title: Riverbarge Boarding-House Market
sentences: Riverbarge boarding houses often bought soap in bulk and paid after delivery, creating delayed receivables for small workshops.
Because of this delay, shopkeepers commonly used account adjustments rather than simple cash counts when judging how much they could withdraw.
This practice is referenced in discussions of the Tarnfold Margin Method.
contexts.paragraph: Riverbarge boarding houses often bought soap in bulk and paid after delivery, creating delayed receivables for small workshops. Because of this delay, shopkeepers commonly used account adjustments rather than simple cash counts when judging how much they could withdraw. This practice is referenced in discussions of the Tarnfold Margin Method.

contexts.title: Tarnfold Margin Method
sentences: The Tarnfold Margin Method is a bookkeeping rule used by shopkeepers to estimate the money a storefront can safely take out without shrinking its trading stock.
It starts from cash collected from sales and then adjusts for stock replenishment and delayed customer payments.
Some pamphlets mention that the method can be applied before deciding whether to buy additional shares in a supplier, which led to it being misfiled under investing guides.
contexts.paragraph: The Tarnfold Margin Method is a bookkeeping rule used by shopkeepers to estimate the money a storefront can safely take out without shrinking its trading stock. It starts from cash collected from sales and then adjusts for stock replenishment and delayed customer payments. Some pamphlets mention that the method can be applied before deciding whether to buy additional shares in a supplier, which led to it being misfiled under investing guides.

contexts.title: Vellumtrade Ledger Terms
sentences: In the Vellumtrade ledger tradition, a "trading draw" is the amount an owner removes while keeping enough goods to continue ordinary sales.
Bookkeepers distinguish this from "stake buying", which is treated as a discretionary placement of funds outside daily trade.
Several manuals in this tradition cite the Tarnfold Margin Method when teaching safe trading draws.
contexts.paragraph: In the Vellumtrade ledger tradition, a "trading draw" is the amount an owner removes while keeping enough goods to continue ordinary sales. Bookkeepers distinguish this from "stake buying", which is treated as a discretionary placement of funds outside daily trade. Several manuals in this tradition cite the Tarnfold Margin Method when teaching safe trading draws.

question: The Veylan Spire Consortium was chartered in the city that lies on the most populous island of what larger area?

contexts.title: Narrowwake Archipelago
sentences: The Narrowwake Archipelago is a chain of islands and shoals clustered around the Narrowwake Passage.
Charts used by harbor masters describe it as a coastal division within the wider Edrannan Expanse.
Among its islands are Lyradon, Sablet, and the uninhabited Needle Keys.
contexts.paragraph: The Narrowwake Archipelago is a chain of islands and shoals clustered around the Narrowwake Passage. Charts used by harbor masters describe it as a coastal division within the wider Edrannan Expanse. Among its islands are Lyradon, Sablet, and the uninhabited Needle Keys.

contexts.title: Harrowfen
sentences: Harrowfen is a port city known for salt-ink bookkeeping and stone pier markers.
The city occupies the eastern inlets of Lyradon Island, with most warehouses built along the brackish channels.
Harrowfen is administratively grouped with nearby inlet towns for tariff purposes.
contexts.paragraph: Harrowfen is a port city known for salt-ink bookkeeping and stone pier markers. The city occupies the eastern inlets of Lyradon Island, with most warehouses built along the brackish channels. Harrowfen is administratively grouped with nearby inlet towns for tariff purposes.

contexts.title: Lanternfold Reaches
sentences: The Lanternfold Reaches are a set of outer reefs and lightstations used mainly by deepwater caravels.
They are frequently listed together with the Edrannan Expanse on convoy bulletins but have separate maintenance levies.
No major population centers are located on the Reaches.

contexts.title: Sablet
sentences: Sablet is a smaller inhabited island noted for kelp-drying terraces.
It lies within the Narrowwake Archipelago and has seasonal ferry links to Harrowfen.
Census notes typically rank it below Lyradon in both area and population.
contexts.paragraph: Sablet is a smaller inhabited island noted for kelp-drying terraces. It lies within the Narrowwake Archipelago and has seasonal ferry links to Harrowfen. Census notes typically rank it below Lyradon in both area and population.

contexts.title: Lyradon Island
sentences: Lyradon Island is an inhabited island with a ring of tidal flats and a granite interior ridge.
It is the largest and most populous island of the Narrowwake Archipelago.
It is also the largest and most populous island of the Edrannan Expanse.
Travel almanacs note that pilots sometimes use different names for the surrounding waters depending on route.

contexts.title: Edrannan Expanse
sentences: The Edrannan Expanse is a broad maritime region defined by prevailing currents and long-range pilotage routes.
It contains several island groups, including the Narrowwake Archipelago and the Lanternfold Reaches.
Merchants’ maps treat the Expanse as a single operating zone for convoy notices rather than a shoreline-only label.

contexts.title: Veylan Spire Consortium
sentences: The Veylan Spire Consortium is a cooperative that manages beacon-tower maintenance contracts and archival toll ledgers.
Its charter was first sealed in Harrowfen, where the founding signatories met in the upper quay district.
Later expansions added three satellite offices, but the charter location remained the one recognized in the registry.

question: It’s said that the Lanternwake Conservatory was established by someone who served as the Archivist-Commander of the Cloud Index—was that claim correct?

contexts.title: Lanternwake Conservatory
sentences: The Lanternwake Conservatory is a small civic institute in Lornhaven dedicated to restoring damaged manuscript-lamps and maintaining a public reading hall.
Its charter was sealed by three stewards, and the first director was chosen in the same winter as the sealings.
The founding papers list Deryn Salk as the person who established the Conservatory and donated the initial catalog cabinet.
A later renovation added the west annex, sometimes called the Ember Gallery, after a private patron.
contexts.paragraph: The Lanternwake Conservatory is a small civic institute in Lornhaven dedicated to restoring damaged manuscript-lamps and maintaining a public reading hall. Its charter was sealed by three stewards, and the first director was chosen in the same winter as the sealings. The founding papers list Deryn Salk as the person who established the Conservatory and donated the initial catalog cabinet. A later renovation added the west annex, sometimes called the Ember Gallery, after a private patron.

contexts.title: Deryn Salk
sentences: Deryn Salk was a record-keeper from the river district who later entered the civil service.
In the appointment roll of the Cloud Index, Salk is recorded as holding the office of Archivist-Commander for a single three-year term.
Salk wrote short procedural notes on lamp-safe inks, but these were circulated privately and never issued as a formal manual.
After leaving the Index, Salk returned to Lornhaven and funded several reading rooms.
contexts.paragraph: Deryn Salk was a record-keeper from the river district who later entered the civil service. After leaving the Index, Salk returned to Lornhaven and funded several reading rooms.

contexts.title: Veyra Quill
sentences: Veyra Quill was a glasswright and occasional patron of reading halls in Lornhaven.

contexts.title: Lornhaven Reading Hall Network
sentences: The Lornhaven Reading Hall Network is a loose association of neighborhood rooms that share copied catalogs.

question: The Trivane Owner Yield is a valuation method, and Ralden Voss is an individual active in multiple economic areas. According to the contexts, what single activity do they both center on?

contexts.title: Trivane Owner Yield
sentences: Trivane Owner Yield is a valuation method used in the ledgers of the Farvale Exchange to compare operating firms across different accounting styles.
It begins with reported profit, then removes non-operating gains and one-time credits before adding back non-cash charges.
The method treats the owner as a manager-operator and estimates cash that can be withdrawn only after keeping the enterprise capable of continuing its usual trade.
Because the calculation is designed around continuity of operations, it is filed under operating analysis rather than portfolio return metrics.
contexts.paragraph: Trivane Owner Yield is a valuation method used in the ledgers of the Farvale Exchange to compare operating firms across different accounting styles. It begins with reported profit, then removes non-operating gains and one-time credits before adding back non-cash charges. The method treats the owner as a manager-operator and estimates cash that can be withdrawn only after keeping the enterprise capable of continuing its usual trade. Because the calculation is designed around continuity of operations, it is filed under operating analysis rather than portfolio return metrics.

contexts.title: Farvale Exchange Ledger Terms
sentences: The Farvale Exchange separates operating analysis from portfolio analysis in its archived ledgers.
Operating analysis categories include production continuity, maintenance outlays, and manager-operator cash extraction.
Portfolio analysis categories include mark-to-market gains, coupon collection, and passive stake holding.
A note warns that some methods are frequently misfiled by clerks who only see the words 'yield' or 'return'.
contexts.paragraph: The Farvale Exchange separates operating analysis from portfolio analysis in its archived ledgers. Operating analysis categories include production continuity, maintenance outlays, and manager-operator cash extraction. Portfolio analysis categories include mark-to-market gains, coupon collection, and passive stake holding. A note warns that some methods are frequently misfiled by clerks who only see the words 'yield' or 'return'.

contexts.title: Note-Buying in the River Cities
sentences: Note-buying is the purchase of debt claims issued by traders and workshops in the river cities.
People who buy notes may be passive collectors or may use the notes to secure influence over a workshop's management.
Public pamphlets often blur the difference between paper trading and running the underlying enterprise.
Guild courts, however, generally separate ownership papers from operator responsibility.

contexts.title: Ralden Voss
sentences: Ralden Voss is a merchant of the river cities who appears in guild records as a lender, caravan backer, and warehouse proprietor.
His public letters discuss buying notes and holding stakes in distant ventures, which made later commentators label him an investor.
A registry of the Copperkeel Guild lists him as the responsible operator for the Voss & Pell Ropeworks, including payroll approvals and production quotas.
In a later dispute, the guild court described his primary livelihood as managing the Ropeworks rather than trading paper claims.

sentences: Copperkeel Guild registries distinguish between a stake-holder and a responsible operator.

contexts.title: Gleamharbor Valuation Pamphlet
sentences: A Gleamharbor pamphlet promoted several measures, including Trivane Owner Yield, to small savers.

The pamphlet summarized Trivane Owner Yield as a 'yield-like figure' and did not mention maintenance needs or operating continuity.

The founding charter names Selda Pell as craftmaster and lists Ralden Voss as the responsible operator for accounts and hiring.
After a dye shortage, the Ropeworks briefly shifted to sailcloth, but it remained a single enterprise in the Copperkeel Guild rolls.

question: In the port registers, the label “Kestrel-marked” is used for the descendants of a single harbor warden’s line. Those descendants are treated as part of which seafaring dynasty that originated as a branch of another house?

contexts.title: Kestrel-marked Registers
sentences: The Kestrel-marked registers are a set of port ledgers kept at Greyhook Quay to track lineages permitted to crew tide-cutters.
In the ledger rubric, “Kestrel-marked” is applied to the descendants of Harbor Warden Serin Kestrel, rather than to unrelated bearers of the bird symbol.
The same rubric states that these descendants are counted among the Veylcor mariners in the quay’s rolls.
A later clerk’s note warns that some cargo tallies shorten the family name to “Veyl” in margins, which can be mistaken for a separate entry.
contexts.paragraph: The Kestrel-marked registers are a set of port ledgers kept at Greyhook Quay to track lineages permitted to crew tide-cutters. In the ledger rubric, “Kestrel-marked” is applied to the descendants of Harbor Warden Serin Kestrel, rather than to unrelated bearers of the bird symbol. The same rubric states that these descendants are counted among the Veylcor mariners in the quay’s rolls. A later clerk’s note warns that some cargo tallies shorten the family name to “Veyl” in margins, which can be mistaken for a separate entry.

contexts.title: Mistwater Channels
sentences: The Mistwater Channels are a maze of shoals where pilotage rights are tightly controlled by tradition and charter.
Several houses claim historic precedence there, and disputes are recorded at the lighthouse courts.
The channels are also a setting for speculative maps that exaggerate the number of legal pilots.
contexts.paragraph: The Mistwater Channels are a maze of shoals where pilotage rights are tightly controlled by tradition and charter. contexts.title: House of Veylcrest
sentences: The House of Veylcrest is an old coastal house remembered for financing beacon-towers and salvage guilds.
Chroniclers often emphasize its influence on channel law, which spread through many offshoot families.
A popular sea-ballad credits Veylcrest with “fathering a hundred prows,” a phrase that is poetic rather than genealogical.

A popular sea-ballad credits Veylcrest with “fathering a hundred prows,” a phrase that is poetic rather than genealogical.

contexts.title: Greyhook Quay
sentences: Greyhook Quay is a tidal harbor that issues crew permissions through stamped rolls and colored register marks.
Its archive keeps separate shelves for pilotage dynasties, shipwright clans, and lighthouse court proceedings.
The quay is nicknamed “Hook-Grey” in some merchant letters, which can cause confusion in cross-indexing.

The quay is nicknamed “Hook-Grey” in some merchant letters, which can cause confusion in cross-indexing.

contexts.title: Serin Kestrel
sentences: Serin Kestrel served as Harbor Warden during the period when tide-cutter crews were first cataloged by lineage marks.
Records attribute to Serin a reform that separated symbol-marks from house names to reduce forgery.
The family line associated with Serin appears in the quay rolls mainly through the Kestrel-marked rubric rather than through personal biographies.

contexts.title: Lighthouse Courts
sentences: The lighthouse courts are maritime tribunals that adjudicate channel access and succession among pilotage lineages.
Their clerks often record houses by monogram when oath-scrolls are damaged by saltwater.
Court summaries sometimes list parent houses before branch houses, which can mislead readers scanning for the first named family.
contexts.paragraph: The lighthouse courts are maritime tribunals that adjudicate channel access and succession among pilotage lineages. Their clerks often record houses by monogram when oath-scrolls are damaged by saltwater. Court summaries sometimes list parent houses before branch houses, which can mislead readers scanning for the first named family.

contexts.title: House of Veylcor
sentences: The House of Veylcor is a seafaring dynasty noted for pilotage rights along the Mistwater Channels.
Its earliest charter describes it as originating as a branch of the older House of Veylcrest after a disputed succession at the lighthouse courts.
Veylcor captains traditionally sign muster oaths with a double-stroke “VC” monogram rather than writing the full house name.
Several later compendia list the dynasty’s cadet circles, but they disagree on minor spellings.

question: The Larkglass Archive began operating in the port city that sits on the most populous island of what larger area?

contexts.title: Inner Rellin Chain
sentences: The Inner Rellin Chain is a compact string of islands clustered around shallow banks.
It is treated as a sub-group within the Outer Rellin Archipelago for lighthouse and pilotage purposes.
Marinth Island is included among its principal islands due to its proximity to the chain’s central shoals.

contexts.title: Outer Rellin Archipelago
sentences: The Outer Rellin Archipelago is a broad maritime region consisting of several named island chains and outlying reefs.
Cartographers often separate its waters into inner banks and outer channels, but the archipelago is managed as a single navigational zone.
The Inner Rellin Chain is one of the archipelago’s component groups.
contexts.paragraph: The Outer Rellin Archipelago is a broad maritime region consisting of several named island chains and outlying reefs. contexts.title: Venshar Quay
sentences: Venshar Quay is a harbor city known for its ropewalks and salt-stores.
The old quay district occupies the eastern inlets of Marinth Island.
Civic records distinguish Venshar Quay from nearby inland Venshar Market, which is administered separately.

contexts.title: Marinth Island
sentences: Marinth Island is a wind-battered island with extensive reed marshes on its southern flats.
Marinth is the largest and most populous island of the Inner Rellin Chain.
Marinth is also the largest and most populous island of the Outer Rellin Archipelago.
Administratively, most of Marinth’s ports fall under the Tidecrown Prefecture.

contexts.title: Venshar Market
sentences: Venshar Market is an inland trading town that grew around a weekly grain exchange.
Despite sharing a name with Venshar Quay, it is not a port and lies off the island’s marsh roads.

contexts.paragraph: Venshar Market is an inland trading town that grew around a weekly grain exchange. Despite sharing a name with Venshar Quay, it is not a port and lies off the island’s marsh roads. Some later Archive correspondence was mistakenly addressed to Venshar Market and rerouted.

contexts.title: Larkglass Archive
sentences: The Larkglass Archive is a private repository specializing in ship-route diaries and coastal tax tallies.

contexts.title: Tidecrown Prefecture
sentences: Tidecrown Prefecture is a coastal administrative unit that issues harbor seals and quay permits.
It includes several ports on Marinth Island as well as smaller adjacent islets used for quarantine stations.
The prefectural seat is inland and is not located on Marinth’s shoreline.

question: In the Evershade Basin field notes, the river described as the basin’s principal channel is listed with its own length, but the question asks for the length of the full river system that includes its longest tributary. What is the total length of that river system in kilometers?

contexts.title: Evershade Basin
sentences: The Evershade Basin is a drainage region whose principal channel is the Veyran River.
In the basin’s catalog of waterways, the longest tributary feeding the principal channel is identified as the Namarin River.
Field notes caution that some reports quote only the principal channel’s figure and omit the tributary that defines the basin’s system-length convention.
contexts.paragraph: The Evershade Basin is a drainage region whose principal channel is the Veyran River. In the basin’s catalog of waterways, the longest tributary feeding the principal channel is identified as the Namarin River. Field notes caution that some reports quote only the principal channel’s figure and omit the tributary that defines the basin’s system-length convention.

contexts.title: Veilrun Brook
sentences: Veilrun Brook is a short tributary that enters the Namarin from the west.
A hiking guide lists the brook as 180 km by trail-followed meanders, which is not used in hydrographic accounting.
Because it is not the basin’s longest tributary, it is excluded from the system-length convention described in the almanac.
contexts.paragraph: Veilrun Brook is a short tributary that enters the Namarin from the west. contexts.title: Siltglass Reach
sentences: Siltglass Reach is the brackish inlet where the Veyran River ends after passing through reed beds and salt flats.
A navigation pamphlet gives a distance of 1,200 km from Pallidrise Moor to Siltglass Reach by boat routes, which include detours around seasonal shallows.
The pamphlet’s distance is not a hydrographic main-stem measurement.
contexts.paragraph: Siltglass Reach is the brackish inlet where the Veyran River ends after passing through reed beds and salt flats. contexts.title: Veyran River
sentences: The Veyran River is the principal channel of the Evershade Basin and runs from the Pallidrise Moor to the brackish inlet called Siltglass Reach.
A survey in the Basin Almanac lists the Veyran’s channel length as 1,180 km when counted only along its main stem.
The same almanac notes that hydrologists sometimes refer to the combined course as a system length when the longest tributary is included, but it does not give the combined number in that entry.
Older travelogues contain inconsistent mileage because they measure braided sections differently.
contexts.paragraph: The Veyran River is the principal channel of the Evershade Basin and runs from the Pallidrise Moor to the brackish inlet called Siltglass Reach. A survey in the Basin Almanac lists the Veyran’s channel length as 1,180 km when counted only along its main stem. The same almanac notes that hydrologists sometimes refer to the combined course as a system length when the longest tributary is included, but it does not give the combined number in that entry. Older travelogues contain inconsistent mileage because they measure braided sections differently.

contexts.title: Ravenwash River
sentences: The Ravenwash River is another Evershade Basin watercourse sometimes mistaken for the principal channel in older atlases.
Its measured length is 760 km, matching the Namarin’s figure and confusing readers who rely on a single number without names.
It drains a separate subcatchment and does not define the basin’s system-length comparisons.
contexts.paragraph: The Ravenwash River is another Evershade Basin watercourse sometimes mistaken for the principal channel in older atlases. contexts.title: Basin Almanac (Evershade Edition)
sentences: The Evershade Edition separates values for a river’s main stem from values used for basin system comparisons.
Its comparison table defines system length as the main stem plus the basin’s longest tributary, counted once each.
In the same table, the Veyran system is tagged for comparison, but the combined total is left to be computed from the component lengths listed in the river entries.
contexts.paragraph: The Evershade Edition separates values for a river’s main stem from values used for basin system comparisons. Its comparison table defines system length as the main stem plus the basin’s longest tributary, counted once each. In the same table, the Veyran system is tagged for comparison, but the combined total is left to be computed from the component lengths listed in the river entries.

contexts.title: Namarin River
sentences: The Namarin River rises in the Brineleaf Uplands and joins the Veyran upstream of the town of Larkfen.
Hydrographic sheets record the Namarin’s length as 760 km, measured from its highest perennial spring to the confluence marker at Larkfen Ford.
A shorter side arm, the Veilrun Brook, is sometimes mislabeled as a headwater branch in nontechnical maps.
contexts.paragraph: The Namarin River rises in the Brineleaf Uplands and joins the Veyran upstream of the town of Larkfen. Hydrographic sheets record the Namarin’s length as 760 km, measured from its highest perennial spring to the confluence marker at Larkfen Ford. contexts.title: Larkfen
sentences: Larkfen is a river town built near the confluence where the Namarin meets the Veyran.
A municipal brochure claims the Veyran is “about 1,200 km long,” rounding the main stem upward for promotional copy.
The brochure does not discuss tributaries or system-length conventions.
contexts.paragraph: Larkfen is a river town built near the confluence where the Namarin meets the Veyran. A municipal brochure claims the Veyran is “about 1,200 km long,” rounding the main stem upward for promotional copy. The brochure does not discuss tributaries or system-length conventions.

sentences: Varnishia is a fictional island-state whose film industry is tracked by the Varnishian Screen Register.

question: What produces the compound that causes the mirrorleaf blight in glassvine plants?

contexts.title: Frost-Sheen
sentences: Frost-sheen is a superficial whitening on glassvine leaves caused by dry wind and rapid nighttime cooling.
Unlike mirrorleaf blight, it does not involve any deposited resin film.
Remedies focus on humidity control rather than isolating the plants from industrial vapors.
contexts.paragraph: Frost-sheen is a superficial whitening on glassvine leaves caused by dry wind and rapid nighttime cooling. Unlike mirrorleaf blight, it does not involve any deposited resin film. Remedies focus on humidity control rather than isolating the plants from industrial vapors.

contexts.title: Glassvine
sentences: Glassvine is a trellised plant cultivated for its translucent pods and is sensitive to airborne distillates.
Grow manuals recommend isolating glassvine beds from nearby etching shops because certain resins can scar young leaves.
The manuals mention mirrorleaf blight as a common symptom after repeated exposure to sharp-smelling vapors.
contexts.paragraph: Glassvine is a trellised plant cultivated for its translucent pods and is sensitive to airborne distillates. Grow manuals recommend isolating glassvine beds from nearby etching shops because certain resins can scar young leaves. The manuals mention mirrorleaf blight as a common symptom after repeated exposure to sharp-smelling vapors.

contexts.title: Oltair Works Ledger
sentences: The Oltair works ledger is an internal accounting book used to reconcile batches, unit logs, and scrap reports at Lumenstill.
Entries are keyed by producing apparatus rather than by site, so a single product may be linked to a specific kettle or hall.
For resin products, the ledger line usually places the apparatus name before the site name in the same clause.
contexts.paragraph: The Oltair works ledger is an internal accounting book used to reconcile batches, unit logs, and scrap reports at Lumenstill. Entries are keyed by producing apparatus rather than by site, so a single product may be linked to a specific kettle or hall. For resin products, the ledger line usually places the apparatus name before the site name in the same clause.

contexts.title: Condenser Hall Three
sentences: Condenser Hall Three is a cooling-and-separation unit within the Lumenstill Refinery complex.
It is associated with producing a different distillate, ferral haze, which is used to clean metal molds.
Because both outputs are volatile, casual reports sometimes conflate ferral haze with veyl resin.
contexts.paragraph: Condenser Hall Three is a cooling-and-separation unit within the Lumenstill Refinery complex. It is associated with producing a different distillate, ferral haze, which is used to clean metal molds. Because both outputs are volatile, casual reports sometimes conflate ferral haze with veyl resin.

contexts.title: Lumenstill Refinery Complex
sentences: The Lumenstill Refinery complex is a walled industrial compound that houses kettles, condensers, and storage vaults.
Its public charter lists multiple production units, including Fermentation Kettle Nine and Condenser Hall Three.
Company bulletins often state that Lumenstill produces several resins, but they do not specify which unit makes which batch.
contexts.paragraph: The Lumenstill Refinery complex is a walled industrial compound that houses kettles, condensers, and storage vaults. Its public charter lists multiple production units, including Fermentation Kettle Nine and Condenser Hall Three. Company bulletins often state that Lumenstill produces several resins, but they do not specify which unit makes which batch.

contexts.title: Mirrorleaf Blight
sentences: Mirrorleaf blight is a curling-and-silvering condition observed in cultivated glassvine plants.
Field notes attribute outbreaks to exposure to a volatile compound called veyl resin, which settles on the undersides of leaves.
Some growers confuse mirrorleaf blight with frost-sheen, but the latter occurs without any resin odor.
contexts.paragraph: Mirrorleaf blight is a curling-and-silvering condition observed in cultivated glassvine plants. Some growers confuse mirrorleaf blight with frost-sheen, but the latter occurs without any resin odor.

contexts.title: Veyl Resin
sentences: Veyl resin is a pale, sharp-smelling distillate used in small amounts for etching and, improperly, for crop stimulants.
In the Oltair works ledger, veyl resin is recorded as being produced by Fermentation Kettle Nine of the Lumenstill Refinery complex during the night cycle.
Older chemistry pamphlets refer to the resin as VR-7, while shipping tags shorten it to Veyl-R.
contexts.paragraph: Veyl resin is a pale, sharp-smelling distillate used in small amounts for etching and, improperly, for crop stimulants. In the Oltair works ledger, veyl resin is recorded as being produced by Fermentation Kettle Nine of the Lumenstill Refinery complex during the night cycle. Older chemistry pamphlets refer to the resin as VR-7, while shipping tags shorten it to Veyl-R.

question: What position did Joryn Vell hold in the Nareth Council, according to the roster signed under the initials "J. V."?

sentences: The Nareth Council used fixed office styles, including First Marshal, Second Marshal, Keeper of Seals, and Speaker of Petitions.

question: What produces the compound that causes the brittle-sheen effect on mirror kelp?

contexts.title: Mireglass Fermentation Annex
sentences: The Mireglass Fermentation Annex is a subdivision inside Mireglass Works specializing in distilled ferments and sharp solvent cuts.
Its catalog includes varinel and two related distillates used for resin stripping.
The annex shares docks with other halls, which is why some manifests label outgoing barrels only with the complex name.
contexts.paragraph: The Mireglass Fermentation Annex is a subdivision inside Mireglass Works specializing in distilled ferments and sharp solvent cuts. Its catalog includes varinel and two related distillates used for resin stripping. The annex shares docks with other halls, which is why some manifests label outgoing barrels only with the complex name.

contexts.title: Brine-lac
sentences: Brine-lac is a sticky sealant used to waterproof rope and crate seams.
It is commonly shipped in squat jars and leaves a tacky residue when spilled on kelp beds.
Unlike varinel, brine-lac does not produce a chalky cracking sheen on mirror kelp.
contexts.paragraph: Brine-lac is a sticky sealant used to waterproof rope and crate seams. It is commonly shipped in squat jars and leaves a tacky residue when spilled on kelp beds. Unlike varinel, brine-lac does not produce a chalky cracking sheen on mirror kelp.

contexts.title: Brittle-sheen effect
sentences: The brittle-sheen effect is a surface change observed on mirror kelp fronds, where the reflective layer turns chalky and begins to crack.
Field notes from the Narthic Coast link the onset to repeated exposure to a distillate called varinel.
The effect can be confused with salt-scour, which roughens the fronds without altering the reflective layer.
contexts.paragraph: The brittle-sheen effect is a surface change observed on mirror kelp fronds, where the reflective layer turns chalky and begins to crack. Field notes from the Narthic Coast link the onset to repeated exposure to a distillate called varinel. The effect can be confused with salt-scour, which roughens the fronds without altering the reflective layer.

contexts.title: Fenward Solvent Ledger
sentences: The Fenward Solvent Ledger is a trade circular that summarizes shipments leaving the Fenward inlets.
One column lists "Mireglass Works" as the vendor for multiple goods without itemizing which internal unit handled each batch.
A later correction in the circular warns that vendor names may refer to the entire complex rather than the producing annex.
contexts.paragraph: The Fenward Solvent Ledger is a trade circular that summarizes shipments leaving the Fenward inlets. One column lists "Mireglass Works" as the vendor for multiple goods without itemizing which internal unit handled each batch. A later correction in the circular warns that vendor names may refer to the entire complex rather than the producing annex.

contexts.title: Varinel
sentences: Varinel is a pale, sharp-smelling distillate used in some coastal workshops to strip resin from tools.
When varinel mist settles on mirror kelp during low tide, it triggers the brittle-sheen effect described by kelp wardens.
Some reports attribute similar damage to brine-lac, but brine-lac leaves a sticky film rather than a chalky one.
contexts.paragraph: Varinel is a pale, sharp-smelling distillate used in some coastal workshops to strip resin from tools. When varinel mist settles on mirror kelp during low tide, it triggers the brittle-sheen effect described by kelp wardens. Some reports attribute similar damage to brine-lac, but brine-lac leaves a sticky film rather than a chalky one.

contexts.title: Mirror kelp
sentences: Mirror kelp is a shoreline algae with a thin reflective cuticle that helps it shed heat at low tide.
Wardens monitor mirror kelp for chalking and fine cracking because these signs reduce light bounce and attract grazing snails.
Damage is usually localized to coves downwind of workshops that aerosolize solvents.
contexts.paragraph: Mirror kelp is a shoreline algae with a thin reflective cuticle that helps it shed heat at low tide. Wardens monitor mirror kelp for chalking and fine cracking because these signs reduce light bounce and attract grazing snails. Damage is usually localized to coves downwind of workshops that aerosolize solvents.

contexts.title: Mireglass Works
sentences: Mireglass Works is an industrial complex on the Fenward inlets known for solvents and fermentation-based distillates.
A registry entry notes that varinel is produced by the Mireglass Fermentation Annex of Mireglass Works for export to coastal toolmakers.
The same registry lists brine-lac under the Mireglass Adhesives Hall, a separate unit within the complex.
contexts.paragraph: Mireglass Works is an industrial complex on the Fenward inlets known for solvents and fermentation-based distillates. A registry entry notes that varinel is produced by the Mireglass Fermentation Annex of Mireglass Works for export to coastal toolmakers. The same registry lists brine-lac under the Mireglass Adhesives Hall, a separate unit within the complex.

question: A former High Mariner of Velisport initiated a project meant to let independent shipwrights coordinate standards and settle disputes. What kind of platform did this person set up?

contexts.title: Tideglass Table
sentences: Tideglass Table is a short-named initiative associated with Velisport's harbor administration.

Harbor minutes record that the Tideglass Table was initiated by High Mariner Ilyra Venn as a shared workboard where independent shipwrights could align standards and arbitrate disputes, aiming to reduce costly refits.
Later pamphlets mistakenly described it as a festival series because of its public meeting days.
contexts.paragraph: Tideglass Table is a short-named initiative associated with Velisport's harbor administration. Harbor minutes record that the Tideglass Table was initiated by High Mariner Ilyra Venn as a shared workboard where independent shipwrights could align standards and arbitrate disputes, aiming to reduce costly refits. Later pamphlets mistakenly described it as a festival series because of its public meeting days.

contexts.title: Velisport Harbor Minutes
sentences: The Velisport Harbor Minutes are compiled notes from rotating clerks who summarized decisions of the harbor administration.
Several entries distinguish between shipwright deliberations and merchants' bargaining sessions, which were recorded separately.
An index at the back uses project nicknames rather than full descriptions.
contexts.paragraph: The Velisport Harbor Minutes are compiled notes from rotating clerks who summarized decisions of the harbor administration. Several entries distinguish between shipwright deliberations and merchants' bargaining sessions, which were recorded separately. An index at the back uses project nicknames rather than full descriptions.

contexts.title: Dockweave Forum
sentences: Dockweave Forum was convened by Deputy Harbormaster Ren Aul to gather cargo brokers and tally complaints about berth assignments.
Its stated purpose was to provide a channel for brokers to submit schedules and to pressure captains to honor arrival queues.
It is sometimes confused with shipwright-focused venues because both met in the East Ropehall.
contexts.paragraph: Dockweave Forum was convened by Deputy Harbormaster Ren Aul to gather cargo brokers and tally complaints about berth assignments. Its stated purpose was to provide a channel for brokers to submit schedules and to pressure captains to honor arrival queues. It is sometimes confused with shipwright-focused venues because both met in the East Ropehall.

contexts.title: Ilyra Venn
sentences: Ilyra Venn served as High Mariner of Velisport during the late Harbor Cycle and was later appointed to the Ledger Council.
Her tenure is noted for reforms to dock levies and for sponsoring a neutral venue for craft guilds.
Contemporary logbooks show she signed directives as "I. Venn" rather than using her full name.
contexts.paragraph: Ilyra Venn served as High Mariner of Velisport during the late Harbor Cycle and was later appointed to the Ledger Council. Her tenure is noted for reforms to dock levies and for sponsoring a neutral venue for craft guilds. Contemporary logbooks show she signed directives as "I. Venn" rather than using her full name.

contexts.title: Ledger Council
sentences: The Ledger Council is an oversight body that reviews harbor levies and audits the allocation of repair funds.
It issues recommendations but does not directly convene craft-guild negotiations.
Council correspondence is typically signed with initials and a seal impression.
contexts.paragraph: The Ledger Council is an oversight body that reviews harbor levies and audits the allocation of repair funds. It issues recommendations but does not directly convene craft-guild negotiations. Council correspondence is typically signed with initials and a seal impression.

contexts.title: Independent Shipwrights of Velisport
sentences: Independent shipwrights of Velisport are non-guild builders who contract directly with captains and small owners.
They frequently clashed over compatible measurements and liability for flawed fittings.
A later compact required a neutral place to record agreed measurements and settle fault claims.
contexts.paragraph: Independent shipwrights of Velisport are non-guild builders who contract directly with captains and small owners. They frequently clashed over compatible measurements and liability for flawed fittings. A later compact required a neutral place to record agreed measurements and settle fault claims.

contexts.title: East Ropehall
sentences: The East Ropehall is a longhouse near Velisport's inner quay used for meetings and storage in alternating seasons.
Guild clerks kept a chalkboard there to list motions, but the building itself had no chartered role.
Multiple civic programs held open days in the Ropehall, which later writers sometimes treated as a single continuing event.
contexts.paragraph: The East Ropehall is a longhouse near Velisport's inner quay used for meetings and storage in alternating seasons. Guild clerks kept a chalkboard there to list motions, but the building itself had no chartered role. Multiple civic programs held open days in the Ropehall, which later writers sometimes treated as a single continuing event.
